title: senecio integrifolius subsp. tundricola
intro: Senecio integrifolius subsp. tundricola, also known as Mountain Ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to mountainous regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Mountain Ragwort include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in some traditional medicine practices. Mountain Ragwort can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy should be carefully evaluated by healthcare professionals.
